title: pelargonium × purum
intro: Pelargonium × purum, also known as the Cape Geranium or Pelargonium sidoides, is a plant species in the Geraniaceae family, native to South Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant has been shown to have various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and aiding in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Pelargonium × purum can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
